tools.description: List the owner's roster (presence-derived MemberDTO[]).
tools.name: get_members

tools.description: Hire a member (server mints the id). Pure seam, no UI (§9.1).
tools.name: hire_member

tools.description: Read one roster member (removed → 404).
tools.name: get_member

tools.description: Edit a member (name / model / effort). Blank name / bad effort → 422.
tools.name: update_member

tools.description: Activate: write desired_state=online intent (does NOT flip online).
tools.name: activate_member

tools.description: Relocate a member to a machine (placement only; never touches desired_state). Also accepts an outsource-worker id: the same move-one-agent verb relocates the worker.
tools.name: relocate_member

tools.description: Deactivate: desired_state=offline + stamp stopping_since (retains row).
tools.name: deactivate_member

tools.description: Force-stop: robust STOP now, bypassing the graceful-stop grace.
tools.name: force_stop_member

tools.description: report_waking(): stamp the caller's waking + clear recycle markers.
tools.name: report_waking

tools.description: report_stopping(): stamp the caller's stopping_since (graceful stop).
tools.name: report_stopping

tools.description: report_stopped(): anchor the caller's stopped; fire recycle kill.
tools.name: report_stopped

tools.description: restart_self(): self-triggered recycle (online-only 409; min-liveness 429).
tools.name: restart_self

tools.description: Refocus a member's context (online-only, else 409).
tools.name: refocus_member

tools.description: Dismiss a member (soft delete). Pure seam, no UI (§9.1).
tools.name: dismiss_member

tools.description: Post a chat message (sender = verified JWT sub; auto SSE fan-out).

ChatAttachmentInputDTO.description:
An attachment carried on a posted chat message — either INLINE BYTES or a
REFERENCE to a blob already in the shared attachment store.

Inline: ``data_b64`` is the bytes, base64-encoded — a data-URI
(``data:<mime>;base64,....``) OR bare base64 (the handler strips a
``data:...;base64,`` prefix if present). ``mime`` and ``filename`` are
optional: an omitted ``mime`` is sniffed for images (else
``application/octet-stream``); an omitted ``filename`` defaults for pasted
images.

Reference: ``id`` names an EXISTING blob (minted by
``POST /api/chat/attachments`` — the streaming upload seam that keeps file
bytes out of a JSON tool call). The stored blob's mime/filename are
authoritative: ``filename``/``mime`` sent alongside ``id`` are IGNORED (so
the upload response ``{id, mime, filename}`` can be pasted back verbatim).
An unknown ``id`` is a 400; carrying BOTH ``id`` and ``data_b64`` is a 400
(ambiguous intent). An item with neither ``id`` nor ``data_b64`` is
silently dropped (legacy tolerance, unchanged).
ChatAttachmentInputDTO.title: ChatAttachmentInputDTO

tools.name: post_chat

tools.description: List the chat stream (?with=<id>&limit=<n>; oldest→newest). History paging: before_ts + before_id (both together) return the limit messages strictly OLDER than that keyset cursor — a history page NEVER advances the read watermark.
tools.name: get_chat

tools.description: List every attachment of a member's conversations (?with=<member_id>; flattened, sender-labelled, newest→oldest).
tools.name: get_chat_attachments

tools.description: Mark a conversation read up to a watermark (reader = verified sub).
tools.name: post_chat_mark-read

tools.description: List chat read receipts (?with=<peer>; per-conversation watermark).
tools.name: get_chat_reads

tools.description: Open a reply card: an ask the owner must answer (options ≤4, [0]=AI pick). Auto-binds to your single active task's current step when unambiguous — that step (and usually the task) enters waiting_owner until the owner answers. Optional attachments ride the question (same shape as post_chat: {id} from `ocagent upload` / POST /api/chat/attachments, or inline data_b64).

REFERENCE to a blob already in the shared attachment store.

Inline: ``data_b64`` is the bytes, base64-encoded — a data-URI

ChatAttachmentInputDTO.title: ChatAttachmentInputDTO

tools.name: create_reply_card

tools.description: List reply cards — LIGHT rows: summary (title) + decision digest only, NO body/options full text (?status=waiting longest-first | answered last-24h; ?limit=N caps the rows). Full card via get_reply_card.
tools.name: list_reply_cards

tools.description: Read one reply card (full context: options, status, answer).
tools.name: get_reply_card

tools.description: Ingest an agent's context gauge (in-memory; bad body → 400).
tools.name: ingest_agent_context

tools.description: Ingest warden telemetry (hardware/limits/tokens/cost/self_update).
tools.name: ingest_telemetry

tools.description: Monitoring telemetry (roster + context + warden push; honest — else).
tools.name: get_monitoring

tools.description: Set an account's display name (id = stable tag). Blank name → 422.
tools.name: update_account

tools.description: Set a machine's display name (id = stable host). Blank name → 422.
tools.name: update_machine

tools.description: List machines (active wardens): machine_id/display_name/online.
tools.name: list_machines

tools.description: Uninstall a machine: drive the uninstall RPC to its warden.
tools.name: uninstall_machine

tools.description: Delete a machine: soft-delete its warden record (no command sent).
tools.name: delete_machine

tools.description: Read the user-custom additive context block (empty = is_default).
tools.name: get_global_context

tools.description: Whole-block replace of the user-custom additive block ({text}).
tools.name: replace_global_context

tools.description: Reset the user-custom block to empty (idempotent tombstone).
tools.name: reset_global_context

tools.description: List role definitions (seed defaults + owner edits).
tools.name: list_roles

tools.description: Create a custom role + its founding member (one pair per call).
tools.name: create_role

tools.description: Read one role definition (unknown → 404).
tools.name: get_role

tools.description: Edit a role definition ({name?, definition_md?}; locked names skip).
tools.name: update_role

tools.description: Reset a role definition to seed (idempotent tombstone overlay).
tools.name: reset_role

tools.description: Hard-delete a custom role + its members (seed → 403; online → 409).
tools.name: delete_role

tools.description: Read a per-role lessons doc (per role_key; overlay ⊕ seed).
tools.name: get_lessons

tools.description: Whole-doc replace of a per-role lessons doc ({text}).
tools.name: replace_lessons

tools.description: Patch a per-role lessons doc by unique anchors ({edits:[{old,new}]}).
tools.name: patch_lessons

tools.description: Bounded LIGHT wake snapshot for the caller (identity-locked; recent chat + light open-task rows + size overview — peek sizes first, pull detail via get_task).
tools.name: resume_summary

tools.description: Size-only PEEK of the wake snapshot (identity-locked; overview counts/sizes + estimated_total_chars, NO chat/task content). Step one of the two-step boot: call this FIRST to size resume_summary, then either call resume_summary directly (small) or hand the pull to a cheap sub-agent that returns a digest (large).
tools.name: peek_resume_summary_size

tools.description: List tasks (?executor=&type=&status=; LIGHT list items — id/task_no/title/type_key/status/priority/executor/creator_id/progress/timestamps/deps, WITHOUT steps/description/inputs). Call get_task for a task's full detail (steps, description, inputs).
tools.name: list_tasks

tools.description: Create a task (dedupes on the manual's key; ad-hoc when type_key omitted). Pass target.kind=outsource to drop the task as an unassigned outsource task (發包); the existing outsource scheduler then spawns workers against the global concurrency cap (outsourceParallelCap) — below the cap it starts immediately, at the cap it queues for capacity and is picked up automatically when a slot frees. No owner-approval card and no per-task approval; the owner may reassign a still-queued task at any time.
tools.name: create_task

tools.description: Read one task (steps, deps, progress, gate cards).
tools.name: get_task

tools.description: Set a task's priority (owner any value; the executor high|mid|low on their own task; frozen stays owner-only).
tools.name: set_task_priority

tools.description: Submit/replace the workflow plan (done and answered-card steps are kept).
tools.name: submit_plan

tools.description: RETIRED (task status is derived from its steps): every call is refused with the lever that replaced it and the task is never mutated. Report step status via update_step_status; take over a reassigned task via claim_task; terminate/duplicate have their own actions.
tools.name: update_task_status

tools.description: Mark a task duplicated, pointing at the original (executor/owner; terminal).
tools.name: mark_duplicate

tools.description: Report a step status (pending/in_progress/waiting_external/done). Entering waiting_external requires a non-blank waiting_reason (422 otherwise); the task status is derived from its steps.
tools.name: update_step_status

tools.description: Arm a gate step: opens the reply card the owner must answer. Optional attachments ride the question (same shape as post_chat: {id} from `ocagent upload` / POST /api/chat/attachments, or inline data_b64).

ChatAttachmentInputDTO.title: ChatAttachmentInputDTO

tools.name: open_gate

tools.description: Replace the blocking-deps list wholesale.
tools.name: set_task_deps

tools.description: Report the task's close-out follow-ups done (terminal tasks only; idempotent).
tools.name: report_task_closeout

tools.description: Reassign a task to a member or a fresh outsource worker (the task's executor or an admin; an outsource target lands the task unassigned for the scheduler to spawn under the global parallel cap; enters the reassigning handover state).
tools.name: reassign_task

tools.description: Take over a reassigned task (the new executor claims it): clears the reassigning lock and fires the predecessor worker. The task status stays derived from its steps; only the lock is cleared. 409 if the task is not under the reassigning lock.
tools.name: claim_task

tools.description: Register a deliverable (file, image, or link) onto the task's artifact set — the pinned deliverables shown on the task card. Append-only and repeatable: call it again to pin more. For a file or image, first upload the bytes via the chat-attachments upload to get an attachment id, then call this with kind=file|image and that attachment_id. For a link (e.g. a PR url) call it with kind=link and url — no upload needed. label is an optional display name (a link title such as "PR #123").
tools.name: add_task_artifact

tools.description: Un-pin (remove) one artifact from a task's artifact set — the counterpart to add_task_artifact. You may remove artifacts from a task you are the executor of (the owner/assistant may remove on any task). Give the task id and the artifact id (the id returned when it was added, or from get_task's artifacts). The underlying file blob is left intact; only the pin on the card is removed.
tools.name: remove_task_artifact

tools.description: Outsource worker's claim: read the task bound to the caller.
tools.name: get_my_task

tools.description: List live outsource workers (codename, model, effort, task).
tools.name: list_outsource_workers

tools.description: List task types (match by display_name/purpose; address by type_key).
tools.name: list_task_manuals

tools.description: Create a task type: pass display_name; the server mints and returns the tm- type_key id (legacy explicit type_key still accepted; duplicate → 409; assignee = owner-only).
tools.name: create_task_manual

tools.description: Read one task manual (purpose/fields/SOP/learnings/assignee).
tools.name: get_task_manual

tools.description: Edit a task manual (partial; content fields agent-editable; assignee = owner-only).
tools.name: update_task_manual

tools.description: Whole-doc replace of a type's learnings (task-close write-back).
tools.name: write_task_learnings
